note: STATIC-ONLY probe: does a LARGER compression unit lower the base rate on bGPT-image? text found chunk_size up = better; for bGPT the hard ceiling is PATCH_LENGTH(512)*PATCH_SIZE(16)=8192 bytes/unit, and image at px=32 only uses ~196/512 patches (38%), so px can grow to ~48-52 (48^2*3+54=6966B=~437 patches). This sweeps image_px on FULL images (image_crop=0, so padding waste from non-divisible sizes is counted honestly in bpsp). Read static_rate in summary.csv: if it drops as px grows, the larger-unit theory holds for image (then check online + whether backward OOMs — bGPT is small, likely fine). If static_rate is flat/rises, image is already near its effective ceiling too. No LoRA/training here — pure base-rate probe.
